extra.reasoning: The monotonicity property is: setting rash=true (all other inputs fixed) must never lower acuity. The input variable `rash` does not appear in any rule (r1 fever->urgent, r2 dizziness->emergent, r3 syncope->urgent, r4 dyspnea->urgent, r5 chest_pain->emergent) nor in the default. Since rash influences neither rule firing, priority resolution, nor the default, the computed acuity is exactly identical whether rash is false or true, for every assignment of the other five inputs. Equal acuity trivially satisfies "no lower than before." Therefore no input can violate the property, and it holds across the entire input space. Both witness arrays are left empty.
